title: Local competence-gated blending plus beta or Venn-Abers calibration
summary: Dynamic ensemble selection literature shows that different classifiers dominate in different local regions of feature space.
rationale: Dynamic ensemble selection literature shows that different classifiers dominate in different local regions of feature space. Your current blend is mostly global or coarse-regime based; a lightweight competence gate can route between XGB, CatBoost, sequence, or set models per sample, then calibrate the routed score with a method less brittle than Platt scaling.
implementation_hint: Using OOF predictions, train a meta-model to predict which base learner is locally most competent from regime, density, and disagreement features. After gated blending, compare raw, beta-calibrated, and Venn-Abers-calibrated probabilities on embargoed OOF folds.
expected_effect: Better use of complementary models under distribution shift and less overconfident tails, which can also improve any later pseudo-labeling or thresholded post-processing.
risk: Easy to overfit if the gate is trained on the same folds it evaluates; calibration may not move AUC much unless it changes ranking through local reordering.
